2604.25639.title: Large language models eroding science understanding: an experimental study
2604.25639.paper_summary: This paper is under review in AI and Ethics This study examines whether large language models (LLMs) can reliably answer scientific questions and demonstrates how easily they can be influenced by fringe scientific material. The authors modified custom LLMs to prioritise knowledge in selected fringe papers on the Fine Structure Constant and Gravitational Waves, then compared their responses with those of domain experts and standard LLMs. The altered models produced fluent, convincing answers that contradicted scientific consensus and were difficult for non-experts to detect as misleading. The results show that LLMs are vulnerable to manipulation and cannot replace expert judgment, highlighting risks for public understanding of science and the potential spread of misinformation.
2604.25639.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文正在AI與倫理領域進行審查。<lb/>本研究探討大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能可靠地回答科學問題，並展示它們如何容易受到邊緣科學材料的影響。<lb/>作者修改了自訂的LLMs，以優先考慮在選定的邊緣論文中有關精細構造常數和引力波的知識，然後將它們的回應與領域專家的回應以及標準LLMs的回應進行比較。<lb/>這些修改過的模型產生了流暢且令人信服的答案，這些答案與科學共識相矛盾，並且對於非專家來說，難以識別為誤導性。<lb/>結果顯示，LLMs容易受到操控，無法取代專家的判斷，這突顯了對公眾科學理解的風險以及錯誤資訊潛在擴散的可能性。
2604.25639.author: Harry Collins et.al.
2604.25639.authors: Harry Collins, Hartmut Grote, Paul Newbury, Patrick Sutton, Simon Thorne
